title: Topology Declarations, Property Equivalence, Policies, and Ownership · Contract Lens: Property Equivalence
summary: Multiple services declare the same queue with different durability and arguments, hardcode every operational x-argument, delete shared topology at startup, and turn precondition failures into reconnect loops. Planned artifact: Add and verify the property equivalence increment in versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Make repeated declarations equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and diagnose precondition mismatch.
Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Property Equivalence · Theory Property Equivalence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8688 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to protect property equivalence and route mutable operations through policy or versioned migration; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Make repeated declarations equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and diagnose precondition mismatch.
